text.title: ci-prod-readiness-gates Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the ci-prod-readiness-gates methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Apply per repo/surface; never bundle multiple unrelated repos into one gate config.
rule.rationale: Mixed repos produce noisy blockers and unclear ownership.

rule.statement: Every gate threshold is a typed value sourced from a budget file (perf_budget.bundle_kb_max etc.). No magic numbers in YAML.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded magics decay; sourced thresholds version with the budget.

rule.statement: Each gate names a single accountable owner. No 'team' / 'we' / empty.
rule.rationale: Without a named owner, broken gates rot.

rule.statement: Budget file carries semver + last_reviewed; stale (>90 days) thresholds are flagged.
rule.rationale: Thresholds decay as the product changes.

rule.statement: Gates ship in shadow (advisory) mode for >= 14 days before becoming blocking. Skipping shadow risks fleet-wide CI breakage.
rule.rationale: Empirical noise rate must be measured before mandating green.

rule.statement: Any blocking-gate exception requires a labeled PR (`gate-exception`) + an audit log entry naming the gate and the override reason.
rule.rationale: Silent overrides destroy trust in the gate.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
